famous quote:
If you want to change the world, start with yourself.
-Mahatma Gandhi
Interview Text:
Samuel G.

(part 1)

I met Samuel when my friend Josh Gwyn (jgwynart.com), who knew I was looking for miracles, texted saying he had a friend in town who had seen tons of miracles. I showed up and a tall, well-built, joy-filled black man gave me a huge, long hug.

Here is Samuel's story. 

I ask him 'how do you hear God to know how and when to heal?'

He said that sometimes he hears a little voice. Like an inner audible voice telling him to do something. "Often I didn't want to do it," he said.

"One time a friend I was in a Bible study with had a sinus infection. I had a vision of putting my mouth over his nose and blowing. It was like an animated Gif in front of my face, wherever I looked. It was in front of my vision. Samuel felt like "No way God. That's crazy." 

After the Bible study another friend of his said, Samuel I feel like God wanted you to do something in there. Samuel told him his crazy vision and the guy was like, "Yep. Okay," as if it weren't crazy. 

Then when the friend with the sinus infection came out they told him about it and he too was like, "yep, okay." 

So Samuel put his entire mouth over his nose and blew. And after that the guy took a breath and said I can breathe again! He couldn't breathe at all beforehand.

"How do you get to the place where you can hear that voice?"

He says it starts with "Okay God, I thought I heard you. Now, I can move on what I heard or I can sit on it." Sometimes it's like he's grabbing my shirt and saying, go talk to that person. Other times I feel like he's telling me to do something and I just have to choose if I'm going to act on it.

He gives me an example. He said one day he was praying for a friend at his school who he didn't see very often. And he felt like God said that she had lost her job and he should pray for that.

The next day he happened to run into her, which had been rare lately. And he said he had been praying and had felt like she had lost her job recently and he wants you to know it's going to be okay. 

She burst into tears because she had lost her job the day before and didn't know what to do. 

Samuel smiles and says, 'I didn't know what to do, being a 6'5" African American male across from this little white girl who was weeping.'
